response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned full-stack engineer with strong systems instincts, low-latency D-language work at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP req/s), and meaningful geospatial experience via GISCollective and Green Map. However, the role explicitly requires modern C++ or Rust, and the resume shows only a brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments over a decade ago (2014-2015) and no Rust. Recent work is TypeScript/Node-heavy, and there is no evidence of routing tile pipelines or navigation guidance QA. Worth a screening call to probe C++ depth and willingness to ramp, but a strong hire is unlikely without more recent systems-language work.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Green Map
Strong engineering discipline: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development
concerns: No modern C++ experience beyond a brief 2014-2015 stint; no Rust at all
No navigation guidance or routing-tile pipeline experience
Currently on a career gap (caregiving) since October 2025
